steps.title: Locate the median positions in data set A
steps.content:
From the histogram the bar heights are 4, 7, 27, 12 across the bins [30,40), [40,50), [50,60), [60,70) — a total of 50 players. The median of 50 values is the average of the 25th and 26th values. Cumulative counts:
• Through [30,40): 4
• Through [40,50): 11
• Through [50,60): 38
Positions 25 and 26 fall between 11 and 38, so both lie in the bin [50,60).

steps.title: Evaluate Statement I (median)
steps.content: Adding the value 18 (smaller than every entry in A) places it first in sorted order, shifting every original value up one position. The median of the 51-value set B is the 26th value of B, which equals the 25th value of A. For Statement I to be forced, we would need $v_{25} < \frac{v_{25}+v_{26}}{2}$, i.e. $v_{25} < v_{26}$. But $v_{25}$ and $v_{26}$ both sit in [50,60), so they could be equal integers, making the two medians equal. Statement I is not guaranteed.

steps.title: Evaluate Statement II (mean)
steps.content: Let $S$ be the sum of A. Then mean$_A=\frac{S}{50}$ and mean$_B=\frac{S+18}{51}$. Mean$_B<$ Mean$_A$ when $50(S+18)<51S$, i.e. $S>900$. The smallest possible $S$ comes from putting every value at the bottom of its bin: $$S_{\min}=4(30)+7(40)+27(50)+12(60)=120+280+1350+720=2470.$$ Since $2470>900$, the inequality holds for every data set consistent with the histogram. Statement II must be true.

steps.title: Combine the results
steps.content: Only Statement II is guaranteed, so only II must be true. That matches choice B — B is correct.
